> _Verbatim cross-reference verdict (ours vs orsc vs Jagex ground truth) from the render-fidelity-cross-reference workflow (2026-06-01)._

## VERDICTS — Roofs / Scene pipeline / Raster

Arbiter = Jagex rev-235 clean decompile (`decompiled/normalized-clean/{k,lb,wb}.java`) + the readable deob (`src/client/world/World.java`). Notation: P0=(lx,ly) NW, P1=(lx+1,ly) NE, P2=(lx+1,ly+1) SE, P3=(lx,ly+1) SW. ORSC corner naming p00=P0, p10=P1, p11=P2, p01=P3.

| # | Behavior | OUR logic (file:line) | ORSC logic (file:line) | JAGEX ground truth (file:line) | WINNER | ACTION | Evidence |
|---|----------|----------------------|------------------------|-------------------------------|--------|--------|----------|
| R1 | Roof under-cull predicate (source of the 0x80 bit) | `roof.go:31-34` `underRoofOverlay = overlayDef(ov).tileType==2`; applied `render.go:208,231` | **No under-roof cull at all** (grep of world/scene/stories/view = 0 hits) | `client.java:6952` / deob `Mudclient.java:7222` `(Hh.bb[hx][hy]&128)==0 && yj==0`; bit set at `World.java:979` `tileType_da_N[deco-1]==2 → \|=128` | **ours** | **PORT-OURS-TO-ORSC-GAP** | OURS's predicate is byte-exact to the clean cull. ORSC never hides the host's own roof → host is occluded by his roof / interior reads dark. Forward-port OURS's `HostUnderRoof` + the `!underRoof` gate to ORSC. |
| R2 | Roof ridge cascade — 7-way face emission + diagonal/quad/split topology | `roof.go:398-438` | `roofs.go` switch (the `diag`/`ec` switch ~line "tex :=" onward) | clean `k.java:1597-1702`; deob `World.java:1249-1307` | **agree** | REDUNDANT-BOTH-CORRECT | Mapped all 7 branches: CASE1 `/`NE→{P0,P1,P3}; CASE2 fallback `/`+roof(lx-1,ly-1)==0→{P2,P3,P1}; CASE3 `\`SE→{P3,P0,P2}; CASE4 `\`NW→{P1,P2,P0}; CASE5 level-WE quad{P0,P1,P2,P3}; CASE6 level-NS quad{P3,P0,P1,P2}; CASE7 split flag = !(roof(lx-1,ly-1)>0\|\|roof(lx+1,ly+1)>0). OURS, ORSC and the clean agree on all corner identities + neighbour-test args. |
| R3 | `getWallRoof` swapped-index (existence query `getWallRoof(ly,lx)`) | OURS uses logical `roofAt(lx,ly)` (`roof.go:156-161`) | ORSC uses logical `getWallRoof(x,z)` (`roofs.go`) | clean `k.java:1462` `d(var56,var44)` + `k.java:1803` index `A[q][var1+var2*48]` with swapped quadrant resolution | **agree** | REDUNDANT-BOTH-CORRECT | Proved the double-swap (args reversed AND index `var1+var2*48` vs diag's `var1*48+var2`) CANCELS: roof at logical (X,Z) reads `A[q][X*48+Z]`, same logical layout as all other grids. The deob's literal swap is an obf index-packing artifact, not a world transpose. Both ports' logical (x,z) reads are correct. |
| R4 | Roof corner leveling-to-max + apex raise + wall-top (method428) + +80000 sentinel | OURS computes `wallTop`/`rh` in `buildStory`, leveling in-place (`roof.go:228-304`); does **not** use the +80000 sentinel | ORSC uses the authentic `roofRaisedMarker=80000` accumulating cache (`roofs.go` applyWall/cleanup/rise/strip) | clean `k.java:1340-1525,1755-1765`; deob `World.java:755-760,1100-1136,1215-1222` (method428 +80000; level pass; sentinel strip) | **agree (both correct), ORSC closer to clean mechanism** | REDUNDANT-BOTH-CORRECT | OURS reproduces the same heights via a different bookkeeping (explicit wallTop + raised[] flags) and the FINDINGS clean-bill verifies it per-branch via `rooftrace`. ORSC ports the literal +80000-marker cache. Same geometry; ORSC's is a more literal transcription. |
| R5 | **Multi-story roof height anchor** | OURS `planeWallTopBonus=192` flat per-plane shim (`roof.go:75,210,448`) — FINDINGS #12 flags as a gated approximation | ORSC threads ONE un-reset elevation cache across stories' wall→roof passes (`stories.go:BuildStories`) — the authentic per-floor lift | clean: `World.java:876-880` builds {host,1,2}; the cache (tileElevationCache) is seeded once (plane-0) and never reset → upper floors stack on lower wall-tops | **orsc** | **ADOPT-ORSC** | ORSC reproduces the clean accumulating-cache lift exactly; OURS uses a flat 192×plane shim (gated only because OURS's dump schema carries one plane). This is the rare case ORSC > OURS — adopt ORSC's cache threading. |
| R6 | Roof plane range cap | OURS `maxRoofPlane=3`, loops `plane..3` (`roof.go:62,447`) — builds a plane-3 roof + over-builds upper stories when host≠plane0 | ORSC `storyPlanes(0)={0,1,2}`, only when host==plane0 (`stories.go`) | clean `World.java:876-880`: `buildSection(...,plane)` then `if(plane==0)` build planes 1,2 — **never plane 3** | **orsc** | **ADOPT-ORSC** | OURS's FINDINGS #13 (roof-maxplane-off-by-one) self-flags the plane-3 build the deob never does; OURS also over-builds upper stories when the host is upstairs (clean only builds the host plane in that case). ORSC is correct. |
| R7 | Eave-strut inward nudge (16u) | OURS `strut = isRoofCorner`; nudge on `!strut(...)` (`roof.go:185-188,330-386`) | ORSC `hasRoofStrut = all-4-tiles<=0`; nudge on `hasRoofStrut(...)` (`roofs.go`) | clean `k.java:1528-1590` `if(!a(x,26431,y))` nudge; deob `World.java:392-396,1225-1240` `isRoofCorner` = ANY-of-4-roofed | **agree** | REDUNDANT-BOTH-CORRECT | `hasRoofStrut == !isRoofCorner`; OURS nudges on `!isRoofCorner`. Logically identical, and the per-corner side indices match the clean `var33` block. |
| R8 | Texture U/V axis order (the deob's flagged transpose) | OURS `texSpan` `ti=(v&vmask)+(u>>proj)`, U=a-group, V=b-group, W=c-group (`rasterize.go:506-526,634`) | ORSC `shadeScanlineTexTransp128` `var14[(var3&0x3F80)+(var2>>7)]`, var3=row=b-group, var2=col=a-group (`raster.go:1441-1456,1494`) | clean `wb.a` `k(wb).java:875` `var14[(var3&16256)+(var2>>7)]`; gradient lb.java:1979-1990 | **agree** | REDUNDANT-BOTH-CORRECT — **NOT a transpose** | Both ports map a-group→column(U), b-group→row(V), c-group→denominator(W), byte-identical to the clean texel fetch + the `wb.a` call-arg order (lb.java:2058). The U/V transpose lives ONLY in the readable deob `Scene.java` (RENDER_DEOB_GAPS row) — neither Go renderer inherits it. |
| R9 | Texture projection shift `vp` (viewDist) | OURS resolved `viewDist=9` (texClass shM=7,shN=3,shDN=4) (`rasterize.go:456,476-485`) | ORSC `rot1024VpSrc=9` justified via `setMidpoints m_qd=9` (`scene.go:76`, `types.go:33`) | clean ctor default `lb.java:4681 R=8`; settable `R=var6` at `lb.java:3802`; runtime override path = setMidpoints | **agree (ours==orsc); arbiter ambiguous on 8 vs 9** | REDUNDANT-BOTH-CORRECT (flag) | Two independent ports both resolved vp=9 (strong corroboration of the runtime m_qd=9 override over the ctor's 8). The clean `R=8` is the constructor seed; both ports assert it's overridden to 9 before render. The clean `>>27+this.R` in var27 is a decompiler mis-grouping — both ports resolve it to `>>(vp-5)=>>4`. **NEEDS-RUNTIME-DIFF** to byte-pin 8-vs-9 against a JAR frame, but ours and orsc cannot disagree here. |
| R10 | Painter sort (qsort, far-first descending) | OURS `scene.go:188` descending (matches mudclient204) | ORSC `qsortPolygons` descending on `poly.mT` (`scene.go:738-771`) | clean `lb.java:1345-1381` | **agree** | REDUNDANT-BOTH-CORRECT | Both far-first descending. FINDINGS disc-deob-qsort-inverted notes the readable deob's partition is ascending (a deob transcription error); neither Go renderer inherits it. |
| R11 | Painter overlap test (AABB rectangle overlap) | OURS `facesort.go:300` clean `(Xa,Ya,Xb,Yb)` | ORSC inline `other.maxP6>cur.minP6 && other.minP6<cur.maxP6 && maxP2/minP2 …` (`scene.go:799-805`) | clean `lb.java` overlap family | **agree** | REDUNDANT-BOTH-CORRECT | Both use the clean non-interleaved rectangle test. FINDINGS disc-deob-overlap-args-interleaved notes the readable deob interleaves `(aX,bY,bX,aY)`; neither Go renderer inherits it. (Byte-level `polygonHit1/2` plane-pierce: NEEDS-RUNTIME-DIFF for full confirmation, structure matches.) |
| R12 | Near-plane clip (vertex split at rot1024ZTop) | OURS `scene.go:300-333` (FINDINGS clean-bill) | ORSC `Render` split vs `rot1024ZTop`, project `(c<<vp)/zTop` (`scene.go:650-693`) | clean `lb.java:1749-1794` | **agree** | REDUNDANT-BOTH-CORRECT | ORSC splits against both neighbours, interpolates cx/cy on the crossing edge, projects with vp=9/zTop=5 — matches the classic Scene + OURS. |
| R13 | Roof gouraud-vs-flat shading + transparent-back | OURS `AddFace(verts,tex,magic,magic)` + roof `SetLight(50,50,-50,-10,-50)` (`roof.go:468-471`) | ORSC `insertFace(n,verts,tex,TRANSPARENT)` + `setDiffuseLightAndColor(-50,-10,-50,50,50,true,-98)` (`roofs.go`) | clean `k.java:1602…1693 a(n,verts,tex,12345678,false)` + `k.java:1718 a(-50,50,-10,-50,true,50,-98)` | **agree** | REDUNDANT-BOTH-CORRECT | Both mark roof faces textured-front / TRANSPARENT(12345678)-back = gouraud per-vertex, with the authentic roof light (true = gouraud flag). |

### Loud flags (orsc-vs-Jagex divergences)
- **ORSC-GAP R1 (PB):** ORSC implements **no under-roof cull**. The Jagex client hides plane-0 roofs (and upper walls/roofs) when the host tile's `objectAdjacency & 0x80` is set (`client.java:6952`). OURS has the byte-exact predicate. **Forward-port OURS's `HostUnderRoof`/cull to ORSC** — without it the bot is occluded by its own roof indoors.

### Loud flags (ours-vs-Jagex — ORSC is the more correct one)
- **R5 (PB, multi-story):** OURS's `planeWallTopBonus=192` flat shim is an approximation; ORSC's un-reset accumulating elevation cache (`stories.go`) is the authentic per-floor lift (`World.java` tileElevationCache seeded once, never reset). **ADOPT-ORSC.**
- **R6 (L→PB upstairs):** OURS builds roofs up to `maxRoofPlane=3` and over-builds upper stories when the host is not on plane 0; the clean builds `{host,1,2}` and only stacks 1/2 when `host==plane0`. **ADOPT-ORSC.**

### Net
Roof ridge cascade, getWallRoof swap, leveling/apex/sentinel, eave struts, texture U/V axis, painter sort, overlap test, near-clip, gouraud — **OURS and ORSC both match the Jagex arbiter** (the "U/V transpose" and the qsort/overlap-interleave bugs are defects in the *readable deob Scene.java only*; neither Go renderer carries them, so OURS's "U/V transpose fix" against the deob was real but ORSC was never broken — REDUNDANT-BOTH-CORRECT). The one place **OURS is uniquely correct vs ORSC is the under-roof cull (R1 → PORT-OURS-TO-ORSC-GAP)**; the places **ORSC is uniquely correct vs OURS are the multi-story roof anchor (R5) and plane-cap (R6) → ADOPT-ORSC** (OURS's shims were gated on its single-plane dump schema, which ORSC's architecture sidesteps). R9 (vp=8-vs-9) is the only item needing a JAR-frame runtime diff, and ours/orsc cannot disagree on it (both =9).
